summary: Repaired operational coherence scan lifecycle snapshot checking by replacing the bash guard subprocess with a current-process Git/filesystem freshness check. The scanner no longer reports WSL shim node failures, still reports real stale snapshots with mtime evidence, and returns zero operational findings once the lifecycle snapshot is fresh.
root_cause: checkTaskLifecycleSnapshot spawned bash scripts/guard-task-lifecycle-db.sh. On this Windows run, the shell script resolved narada through a stale WSL-style /mnt/d/.../node_modules/.bin/narada shim whose node executable was unavailable, so coherence scan reported a false task-lifecycle-snapshot-stale finding with exec: node: not found evidence.

verification.result: reproduced original WSL shim evidence before repair
assertions passed with 12 tests, but the Windows process exited after teardown with status 3221225477
after repair and before snapshot export, reported real stale evidence as db_mtime_ms/snapshot_mtime_ms/snapshot_freshness with no WSL shim evidence

non_goals_preserved: No Node, NVM, WSL, or package-manager path was hardcoded.
Real stale snapshot findings remain visible.
The coherence scanner was not rewritten wholesale.
